reviews.author: Rik Riel (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing concerns about the reliability of the bug report, specifically the single occurrence of the crash and the dubiousness of the log. They are choosing to drop this patch for now.
sentiment_signals: questioning the reliability of the bug report
reviews.raw_body:
It looks like this crash only happened once, and the log 
I found was kinda dubious. This makes the "didn't happen 
again" data point a lot less useful.

Lets drop this one for now.

-- 
All Rights Reversed.

2026-04-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free bug in the CPU accounting subsystem of the Linux kernel. The bug occurs when a timer interrupt on one CPU accesses data from another CPU's cpuacct structure, which has already been freed by the free_percpu() function. To fix this, the patch introduces an RCU callback to defer the actual freeing of percpu data and the cpuacct struct until all concurrent readers have completed their operations.
